title: The Future of Healthcare Marketing
summary: This post was created in partnership with Doceree Key takeaways Annual plans, quarterly campaign cycles, and omnichannel strategies make keeping up with the dynamic pace of provider and patient journeys […]

title: Patient messages to providers skyrocket since 2020: study
summary: Between 2020 and 2025, patient-written messages increased 153%, according to the study in JAMA. But office visits also rose, suggesting messaging doesn’t replace in-person care.

title: Upside secures $20M series A to expand healthcare-focused housing platform
summary: The company plans to use the funding simultaneously across Medicaid, Medicare Advantage and employer-sponsored markets.

title: US health spending set to reach $9T by 2034: CMS report
summary: U.S. healthcare spending is set to climb to $9 trillion by 2034, representing 20.6% of the economy, according to new federal data. Actuaries cited prescription drugs as a near-term driver of utilization and cost.

title: Assort Health scores $120M series C to scale voice AI agent platform for healthcare
summary: Assort Health has a large proprietary specialty dataset built on 190 million patient interactions, 62,000 care protocols and 1.6 million decision pathways.

title: Stanford researchers will discuss their agentic 'scientists' that are on course to reshape drug discovery at VB Transform 2026
summary: Drug discovery is notoriously inefficient. Pharmaceutical projects span years, moving from one specialized human team to the next through disconnected workflows that result in knowledge loss during each handoff. A shocking 90% to 95% of drug discovery projects reportedly fail...

title: Cassidy's new plan to overhaul 340B: Rebates, contract pharmacy limits and more changes
summary: A legislative discussion draft shared Thursday by the Senate HELP chairman includes rebates, contract pharmacy limits, narrowed definitions and other proposals.

title: Democrats Demand Answers After White House Denies Trump Received Experimental Weight Loss Drug
summary: The White House denied earlier this week that the president received priority access to an experimental weight loss drug.

title: Patients prefer healthcare providers' AI agents to public chatbots, with human oversight non‑negotiable, survey finds
summary: Patients are open to using AI agents to help with tasks like scheduling and billing, but they overwhelmingly prefer AI tools that live inside their doctor’s secure portal, a new Salesforce survey found.

title: The $50B rural health transformation fund is pushing many hospitals to shrink
summary: To avoid losing funding, many states are pursuing proven cost-saving strategies like downsizing inpatient care rather than untested approaches, some experts say.

title: Patients Have Spoken: 5 Hard Truths About Healthcare Patient Experience in the Age of AI
summary: New global research from Salesforce reveals what patients really want from AI, and the gap between expectation and reality is wider than most providers think.

title: US health spending spikes to $5.7T in 2025, though growth should moderate, CMS finds
summary: Utilization — not cost growth — continues to accelerate spending, government actuaries said. Rising prescription drug spending, including on GLP-1s, is especially acute.

title: Kaiser Health Plan ordered to pay hospital $82M over insufficient reimbursement
summary: A drawn-out legal back-and-forth between Kaiser Health Plan and Pomona Valley Hospital over out-of-network emergency care reimbursement hinged on the terms of a previously terminated contract, making the case "a useful reminder that a terminated contract is not always a dead...

title: Medicare’s AI push snarls patients and doctors in errors and delays
summary: One key concern is that it all happened too hastily. WISeR was announced in June 2025 and launched in mid-January.

title: HCA’s chief clinical officer to exit after 4 years
summary: Dr. Michael Cuffe, who assumed the role in 2022, is leaving at the end of August.

title: DOJ announces $6.5B healthcare fraud takedown with record Medicaid enforcement
summary: Law enforcement unveiled charges against 455 defendants for their alleged participation in healthcare schemes, among which fraudulent amniotic wound allografts and undelivered Medicaid services were spotlighted.

title: Infinitus launches risk detection system to ensure appropriate patient triage from AI agents
summary: The solution—dubbed Clinical Escalations—continuously assesses risk during interactions, allowing reclassification of events to emergent clinical situations.

title: Florida launches probe into CVS for alleged anticompetitive pharmacy practices
summary: The investigation by Florida’s attorney general comes as states look to check pharmacy benefit managers for allegedly driving up drug costs.

title: HHS, responding to China’s rise, moves to fast-track early drug research
summary: A series of newly planned reforms are designed to entice companies to start early trials in the U.S. rather than overseas.

title: FDA drops Whoop warning letter over blood pressure feature
summary: Whoop made changes to its blood pressure insights feature that satisfied the FDA’s requirements for wellness devices.

title: Michigan hospitals denounce legislation to cap prices, limit mergers
summary: A trio of bills introduced by House Speaker Matt Hall would "severely harm hospitals, jeopardize their financial viability and threaten access to care throughout Michigan," the state's hospital association said Monday.

title: Locum tenens coverage becoming ‘more purposeful’ staffing strategy: Definitive Healthcare
summary: Definitive Healthcare identified four key workforce trends around contingent staffing, advanced practice providers, new behavioral health delivery models and AI tech as a workforce multiplier.

title: Badges, bots and blind spots
summary: Hospitals are under pressure to secure increasingly digital care environments without slowing clinicians down. This week on "Podnosis," Senior Writer Anastassia Gliadkovskaya talks with Imprivata Chief Medical Officer Sean Kelly, M.D., about access management, AI risk and the...
